comments.text: Just like McKee, K. Lapkova in [La18] has studied\[\sum_{n\leq N}\tau(n^2+2bn+c)\]There are several partial results given in that paper as well.
As explained in Tao's blogpost or the linked papers, $f$ should be assumed non-constant. Otherwise, $f := 1$ is a boring counterexample. (The site has been updated to address this comment.)

The constant 2 in the final asymptotic is actually more complicated than this, for instance for the polynomial $n^2+1$ it is $\frac{3}{\pi}$ (in general there are terms relating to the class number of the associated quadratic field, as first worked out by McKee). Also one needs to assume $f$ is irreducible, otherwise one instead gets a $X \log^2 X$ type growth. See the discussion at https://github.com/teorth/erdosproblems/issues/84 . (The site has been updated to address this comment.)
I have a blog post on this topic. (The site has been updated to address this comment.)
